procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Mechanical Seal Replacement

steps.instruction: Isolate pump from process and lock out energy sources using lockout hasp and valve lockout devices.
Remove pump casing bolts using combination wrench set and socket set.
Extract old mechanical seal from stuffing box and inspect seal faces for damage.
Clean seal chamber and stuffing box using degreaser solvent and verify no scoring on gasket surfaces.
Install new mechanical seal ensuring proper alignment using dial indicator to verify runout within 0.05mm.
Apply anti-seize compound to casing bolt threads and reinstall pump casing.
Torque casing bolts to specification in star pattern using calibrated torque wrench.
Remove lockout devices and perform leak test under operating pressure.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Bearing Replacement

steps.instruction: De-energize and lock out pump motor using circuit breaker lockout device and verify zero energy.
Remove coupling guard and disconnect motor coupling using hex key set.
Remove bearing housing cover bolts and lift cover using lever hoist.
Extract old ball bearing from shaft and inspect for contamination or wear patterns.
Clean bearing housing thoroughly with degreaser and verify no debris remains.
Pack new bearing with lithium grease using bearing packer tool until grease extrudes.
Install new bearing onto shaft and verify seating using micrometer measurements.
Apply thread locking compound to bearing housing cover bolts and torque to specification in sequential pattern.
Reconnect coupling and check alignment using dial indicator to confirm within 0.1mm tolerance.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Impeller Inspection and Replacement

steps.instruction: Isolate pump from all energy sources and drain process fluid completely using lockout hasp.
Remove pump casing bolts using socket set and combination wrench set.
Lift pump casing using chain hoist with wire rope sling rated for load.
Remove impeller locking nut using pneumatic impact wrench and extract impeller from shaft.
Inspect impeller vanes for cavitation damage and measure wear ring clearances with vernier caliper.
Install new wear ring set if clearances exceed 0.5mm tolerance specification.
Mount new bronze impeller onto shaft and apply anti-seize to locking nut threads.
Torque impeller locking nut to specification using calibrated torque wrench.
Replace spiral wound gasket on casing flange and verify gasket surface is clean and undamaged.
Reinstall casing and torque flange bolts to specification in star pattern using hydraulic torque wrench.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Shaft and Back Plate Assembly

steps.instruction: Lock out all pump energy sources and verify zero energy state with multimeter and pressure gauge.
Drain pump casing completely and purge with compressed air to remove residual fluids.
Disconnect pump from piping and remove all casing fasteners using socket set and adjustable wrench.
Lift pump casing using chain hoist with proper rigging and set aside on protected surface.
Remove rotor assembly including shaft, impeller, and mechanical seal as complete unit using lever hoist.
Inspect back plate and stuffing box gland for cracks or erosion damage using thermal imaging camera.
Install new stainless steel shaft with pre-assembled components and verify concentricity using dial indicator.
Replace back plate with new cast iron component ensuring proper alignment with dowel pins.
Install back plate bolts with spring lock washers and torque to specification in cross pattern.
Reinstall casing with new EPDM gasket and torque casing bolts to specification in star pattern.
Perform vibration analysis baseline measurement and verify readings within acceptance criteria.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Stuffing Box and Gland Packing Service

steps.instruction: Shut down pump and lock out motor using circuit breaker lockout device.
Remove stuffing box gland nuts using combination wrench set and back off gland follower.
Extract old packing rings from stuffing box and clean surfaces with degreaser solvent.
Install brass lantern ring at correct position in stuffing box for flush fluid injection.
Install new PTFE graphite packing rings ensuring staggered joints and proper compression.
Reinstall stuffing box gland and hand-tighten gland nuts using combination wrench set.
Remove lockout device and start pump to break in packing allowing controlled leakage.
Adjust gland compression gradually until leakage rate reaches 40-60 drops per minute.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Mechanical Seal Replacement

steps.instruction: Isolate pump from process and lock out electrical supply using circuit breaker lockout device.
Drain pump casing completely and verify zero pressure using digital pressure gauge.
Remove pump casing bolts using combination wrench set in sequential pattern.
Carefully extract impeller assembly and place on clean work surface.
Remove existing mechanical seal and inspect seal faces for damage or scoring.
Clean stuffing box thoroughly using cleaning solvent and verify bore dimensions with vernier caliper.
Install new mechanical seal ensuring proper alignment using dial indicator with maximum runout of 0.05mm.
Reinstall impeller and back plate assembly onto pump shaft.
Install new gasket on casing flange and position pump casing.
Install casing bolts with anti-seize compound and torque to specification in star pattern.
Remove lockout devices and perform hydrostatic test at 1.5 times operating pressure.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Bearing and Wear Ring Replacement

steps.instruction: Verify pump is properly isolated and locked out per PRC-006 requirements.
Remove coupling guard and measure coupling alignment using dial indicator before disassembly.
Disconnect coupling halves and remove pump shaft assembly from bearing housing.
Remove existing ball bearings from shaft using bearing puller and inspect for contamination or damage.
Clean bearing housing and shaft journals thoroughly with cleaning solvent and inspect for wear.
Pack new bearings with lithium grease using bearing packer tool ensuring complete fill.
Install new bearings onto shaft ensuring proper seating and orientation of seals.
Remove old wear rings from impeller and casing using angle grinder with care to avoid base metal damage.
Install new wear ring set ensuring proper clearances measured with micrometer between 0.25-0.40mm.
Reinstall shaft assembly into bearing housing and secure with housing bolts torqued to specification.
Reconnect coupling and verify alignment within 0.05mm offset and 0.1mm angular using dial indicator.
Lubricate bearing housing through grease fittings with hydraulic grease gun until purge is visible.

procedures.title: Centrifugal Pump Stuffing Box and Lantern Ring Service

steps.instruction: Lock out pump and isolate flush water supply using valve lockout device.
Back off gland bolts evenly using socket set and remove stuffing box gland.
Extract old packing rings and lantern ring using hook tool ensuring complete removal.
Clean stuffing box bore and shaft sleeve with solvent and inspect for scoring or damage.
Install new packing rings in alternating joint pattern with lantern ring positioned at flush port.
Apply thin coat of anti-seize compound to gland bolts and reinstall gland finger-tight.
Restore flush water supply and verify flow through lantern ring port before tightening gland.
Tighten gland bolts evenly in cross pattern to compress packing allowing controlled leakage of 40-60 drops per minute.

procedures.title: Gate Valve Stem and Packing Replacement

steps.instruction: Isolate the valve from system pressure and lock out upstream and downstream isolation points.
Remove bonnet bolts using combination wrench set and lift bonnet assembly clear.
Extract the gate valve stem from the body and inspect for wear and scoring.
Clean the stem cavity and bonnet bore using degreaser solvent and verify dimensional tolerances.
Install new PTFE graphite valve packing in stuffing box ensuring proper compression.
Position bonnet assembly with new gasket and install bonnet bolts finger-tight.
Torque bonnet bolts to specification using star pattern with calibrated torque wrench.
Remove lockout devices and perform valve stroke test under system pressure.

procedures.title: Globe Valve Trim and Seat Overhaul

steps.instruction: Verify system isolation and drain all fluid from valve body before disassembly.
Remove bonnet fasteners and carefully lift bonnet to expose trim components.
Extract globe valve bonnet assembly and inspect internal seating surfaces for damage.
Clean all valve body internal surfaces using appropriate solvent and wire brush.
Measure seating surface flatness using dial indicator to verify within tolerance.
Install new valve packing and bonnet gasket using anti-seize compound on threaded components.
Position bonnet assembly and install bolts hand-tight before applying torque sequence.
Torque bonnet bolts to specification in cross pattern using calibrated torque wrench.
Remove lockout devices and pressure test valve assembly per system requirements.

procedures.title: Gate Valve Internal Inspection and Reassembly

steps.instruction: Apply lockout devices to all energy sources and verify zero pressure with gauge.
Remove all bonnet bolting using socket set and separate bonnet from body.
Inspect gate valve stem threads and stem packing area for wear using micrometer.
Clean all internal components with degreaser and inspect sealing surfaces for scoring.
Apply anti-seize compound to stem threads and install new PTFE packing rings.
Position new spiral wound gasket on body flange face ensuring proper alignment.
Install bonnet and torque flange bolts to specification using star pattern.
Perform leak test at operating pressure and stroke valve through full travel range.

procedures.title: Globe Valve Packing Gland Adjustment and Replacement

steps.instruction: Verify valve is in service position and identify packing leak location.
Attempt to stop leak by tightening gland bolts incrementally using combination wrench.
If leak persists, isolate valve and lock out system using appropriate devices.
Remove gland bolts and extract worn packing rings from stuffing box.
Clean stuffing box bore and stem surface using solvent and inspect for damage.
Install new PTFE graphite packing rings ensuring joints are staggered by 90 degrees.
Install gland follower and torque gland bolts to compress packing evenly.
Remove lockout devices and verify no leakage under operating pressure.

procedures.title: Gate Valve Bonnet Gasket Replacement Under Pressure

steps.instruction: Establish controlled isolation procedure and verify valve integrity before intervention.
Install temporary bolting and studs to maintain gasket compression during replacement.
Sequentially loosen and replace bonnet bolts one at a time using hot-bolt procedure.
Remove old gasket material and clean flange faces without damaging sealing surface.
Install new spiral wound gasket ensuring proper centering and orientation on flange.
Torque all bonnet bolts to specification in star pattern using hydraulic torque wrench.
Monitor flange joint for leakage and re-torque after thermal stabilization if required.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Bearing Replacement

steps.instruction: De-energize motor and apply lockout-tagout to supply breaker.
Remove motor terminal box cover and disconnect leads.
Remove coupling half and extract rotor assembly from stator housing.
Press off worn bearing from rotor shaft using bearing puller.
Clean bearing seats and inspect for damage or contamination.
Heat new bearing to 80°C and press onto rotor shaft.
Reinstall rotor assembly into stator housing and secure end bells.
Reconnect coupling half and verify alignment within tolerance.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Terminal Box Re-wiring

steps.instruction: Verify motor is de-energized and locked out at supply breaker.
Remove terminal box cover and gasket, inspect for damage.
Disconnect existing cable terminations and remove damaged wiring.
Route new cables through gland and terminate to stator winding leads.
Perform insulation resistance test on all three phases to ground.
Install new terminal box gasket and secure cover.
Verify phase rotation and remove lockout-tagout devices.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Alignment to Driven Equipment

steps.instruction: Verify motor is de-energized and locked out.
Loosen motor foundation bolts and position dial indicators on coupling.
Rotate coupling and record radial and axial runout measurements.
Adjust motor position using shims to achieve parallel alignment within 0.05mm.
Adjust motor position laterally to achieve concentric alignment within 0.05mm.
Torque motor foundation bolts to specification in cross pattern.
Perform final alignment verification and document results.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Insulation Resistance Testing

steps.instruction: Verify motor is de-energized and all personnel are clear.
Disconnect all motor leads from supply cables and isolate.
Connect insulation tester leads to Phase U and motor frame ground.
Apply 500V DC test voltage for 60 seconds and record insulation resistance.
Repeat insulation test for Phase V and Phase W to ground.
Perform phase-to-phase insulation resistance tests on all combinations.
Discharge windings, reconnect motor leads, and document test results.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Vibration Analysis and Diagnosis

steps.instruction: Verify motor is running at normal operating conditions for minimum 30 minutes.
Attach vibration analyzer sensors to motor drive-end bearing housing.
Record vibration spectra in horizontal, vertical, and axial directions.
Attach sensors to motor non-drive-end bearing housing and repeat measurements.
Perform thermal imaging scan of motor housing and terminal box.
Analyze frequency spectra for bearing defects, misalignment, and unbalance signatures.
Compare readings to baseline and ISO standards, document findings and recommendations.

procedures.title: Induction Motor Decoupling for Shop Overhaul

steps.instruction: De-energize motor and apply lockout-tagout to all isolation points.
Remove terminal box cover and disconnect all supply cables.
Mark coupling alignment positions and remove coupling guard.
Remove coupling fasteners and separate coupling halves.
Attach chain hoist to motor lifting eyes and take up slack.
Remove motor foundation bolts and washers.
Lift motor clear of foundation and position on transport skid.
Secure motor to transport skid with fasteners and prepare for shipment.
Install blanking covers on driven equipment coupling and cable terminations.

procedures.title: Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchanger Tube Bundle Removal

steps.instruction: Isolate heat exchanger from process lines and confirm zero energy state with lockout devices.
Drain all fluid from shell and tube sides, and verify complete drainage.
Remove channel head flange bolts using impact wrench and store hardware systematically.
Attach chain hoist to channel head lifting lugs and remove channel head assembly.
Inspect and photograph tube sheet face and gasket seating surfaces for damage.
Attach lifting sling to tube bundle and carefully withdraw bundle from shell using hoist.
Place tube bundle on designated clean work surface and secure for subsequent cleaning operations.

procedures.title: Heat Exchanger Tube Bundle Cleaning and Inspection

steps.instruction: Clean exterior of tube bundle with degreasing solvent and compressed air to remove loose deposits.
Mechanically clean individual tubes using approved tube cleaning brushes and verify through-passage.
Inspect each tube for corrosion, erosion, and damage using visual and eddy current methods.
Clean tube sheet face and baffle plates thoroughly, removing all gasket residue and deposits.
Measure and document tube sheet flatness using dial indicator at specified grid points.
Apply protective coating to cleaned tube bundle and cover to prevent contamination.

procedures.title: Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchanger Gasket Replacement and Assembly

steps.instruction: Verify all gasket seating surfaces are clean, dry, and free from damage or scoring.
Install new spiral wound gasket on tube sheet flange face, ensuring proper centering and alignment.
Position cleaned tube bundle into shell using chain hoist, ensuring alignment with shell nozzles.
Install channel head with new gasket, aligning bolt holes and inserting guide studs.
Install all flange bolts with anti-seize compound on threads and verify correct quantity and length.
Torque flange bolts to initial pass at 30% of final torque in star pattern using calibrated wrench.
Torque flange bolts to second pass at 70% of final torque in star pattern.
Torque flange bolts to final specification in star pattern and mark witness marks.
Verify all bolts achieve final torque and check for uniform gasket compression visually.

procedures.title: Heat Exchanger Hydrostatic Test and Return to Service

steps.instruction: Install temporary test blinds and fill heat exchanger shell side with calibrated test water slowly.
Vent all air from system and verify complete filling with liquid.
Pressurize shell side to 1.5 times design pressure using calibrated test pump.
Hold test pressure for 30 minutes and monitor for pressure drop or visible leakage.
Inspect all flanged joints, welds, and tube-to-tubesheet connections for leaks during hold period.
Depressurize system slowly and drain test fluid completely from heat exchanger.
Repeat hydrostatic test procedure for tube side at 1.5 times design pressure.
Remove test blinds, dry heat exchanger internals, and install permanent instrumentation.
Remove lockout devices, restore heat exchanger to operational configuration, and document completion.

procedures.title: Reciprocating Compressor Reed Valve Replacement

steps.instruction: Isolate compressor from all energy sources and apply lockout devices to power supply and isolation valves.
Vent compressor cylinder to atmospheric pressure and verify zero pressure using digital gauge.
Remove cylinder head bolts in reverse star pattern and lift head using lever hoist.
Extract old reed valve set and valve plate assembly from cylinder head.
Clean valve seat surfaces with degreaser solvent and inspect for damage or scoring.
Install new reed valve set and valve plate assembly with new gasket.
Position cylinder head with new gasket and install bolts finger-tight.
Torque cylinder head bolts to specification in star pattern sequence.
Remove lockout devices and restore compressor to service following startup procedure.

procedures.title: Reciprocating Compressor Piston Ring Replacement

steps.instruction: Implement complete lockout-tagout on compressor electrical supply and all process isolation valves.
Drain compressor crankcase oil completely and capture for disposal or analysis.
Remove cylinder head assembly per previously completed valve service procedure.
Disconnect connecting rod from crankshaft and extract piston assembly from cylinder bore.
Remove piston pin and separate piston from connecting rod assembly.
Remove old piston ring set and clean piston ring grooves thoroughly with solvent.
Measure cylinder liner bore diameter at multiple positions using micrometer to verify wear limits.
Install new piston ring set ensuring correct orientation and ring gap positioning.
Reassemble piston to connecting rod with new piston pin and verify clearances.
Lubricate cylinder bore and piston rings with compressor oil and carefully insert piston assembly.
Reconnect connecting rod to crankshaft and torque fasteners to specification.
Reinstall cylinder head assembly with new gasket and torque per specification.
Refill crankcase with specified grade and quantity of compressor lubricating oil.
Remove all lockout devices and perform dry rotation test before pressurization.

procedures.title: Reciprocating Compressor Lubrication System Service

steps.instruction: Shut down compressor normally and lock out main electrical supply.
Drain oil from crankcase sump and collect sample for laboratory analysis.
Remove and clean crankcase magnetic drain plug and inspect for metal contamination.
Check oil pressure relief valve operation and clean or replace as necessary.
Inspect sight glass for clarity and check oil level indicator for proper operation.
Service lubrication system strainer and replace filter element if fitted.
Refill crankcase with manufacturer-specified lubricant to proper level.
Lubricate all accessible grease points on crosshead guides and linkages using grease gun.
Remove lockout and start compressor to verify oil pressure within normal operating range.

procedures.title: Pressure Transmitter Calibration and Verification

steps.instruction: Place control system in manual mode and isolate transmitter from process using block valves.
Vent transmitter sensing line to atmosphere and verify zero pressure condition.
Connect calibrated pressure gauge in parallel with transmitter for reference measurement.
Verify and adjust zero reading on transmitter output at atmospheric pressure condition.
Apply full-scale pressure using calibrated source and verify transmitter span accuracy.
Perform five-point calibration check at 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100% of span.
Document as-found and as-left calibration values on instrument data sheet.
Remove calibration equipment and restore transmitter to process service with isolation valves.

procedures.title: Temperature Transmitter Loop Integrity Check

steps.instruction: Verify control system can accept manual input and document current transmitter reading.
Disconnect RTD sensor leads at transmitter terminal block and verify wiring integrity.
Measure RTD resistance at ambient temperature and compare to PT100 reference table.
Simulate temperature signal using precision decade box at low, mid, and high range values.
Verify transmitter output signal corresponds correctly to simulated temperature input.
Reconnect RTD sensor leads and verify proper terminal tightness and wire dress.
Confirm transmitter returns to normal process reading and record final loop check results.

procedures.title: Control Valve Stroke Test and Actuator Inspection

steps.instruction: Place control loop in manual mode at current valve position and notify operations.
Isolate valve actuator air supply and depressurize diaphragm chamber to verify fail-safe action.
Restore air supply and stroke valve from 0% to 100% command in 25% increments.
Measure and record stem travel at each commanded position using dial indicator.
Inspect actuator diaphragm for cracks, tears, or degradation through inspection ports.
Check valve packing gland for leakage and adjust gland nuts if excessive stem leakage observed.
Lubricate valve stem and packing if accessible using appropriate valve lubricant.
Return valve to pre-test position and restore control loop to automatic mode.

procedures.title: Differential Pressure Transmitter Zero and Span Adjustment

steps.instruction: Place associated control loop in manual mode and document current transmitter output.
Close high and low side isolation valves and open equalization valve to zero differential.
Vent both high and low pressure sensing chambers to eliminate trapped gas or liquid.
Verify zero differential condition using reference gauge and adjust transmitter zero if required.
Close equalization valve and apply known differential pressure using calibrated pressure source.
Verify transmitter span accuracy at full scale and adjust span setting if deviation exceeds tolerance.
Perform linearity check at 25%, 50%, and 75% of calibrated span range.
Remove calibration equipment, open isolation valves, close equalization valve per proper sequence.
Restore transmitter to service and verify stable process reading before returning loop to automatic.
